label: dpfi_initial_condition_suite_v1_orca_fusion_m10
purpose: exercise the sealed historical ORCA-Fusion M=10 dual-port Faraday inverse on source-traceable Oxford/Robert-group conditions and representative 166Er analytic morphology surrogates
status: planned synthetic method-development replay; no accepted result set has been generated, and the source inverse is not the active 166Er performance contract

observable_usability.decision_rule: each observable is usable independently when its dimensionless score is <= 1; no overall image-usability score is defined
observable_usability.truth_contract: truth and reconstruction observables are evaluated on the same fixed inverse support without pupil filtering

ensemble.status: one deterministic draw per condition and fluence is an end-to-end model sweep, not an uncertainty or coverage study

initial_conditions.parameter_status: identical to the existing repository reference condensate; Oxford Trap-I pure-BEC contact-TF interpretation
initial_conditions.source_label: Existing repository reference condensate, using the Oxford Trap-I parameters reported by Krstajic et al. 2025
initial_conditions.relationship_to_existing_reference: This is the existing reference condensate, not an additional independent initial condition.
initial_conditions.description: The existing smooth 25k-atom reference and the Oxford reference condensate core are the same benchmark input. It represents only the condensate component of the finite-temperature Oxford sample and uses a contact-TF profile rather than a dipolar equilibrium solution.

initial_conditions.parameter_status: published raw-data row; analytic contact-TF core plus a population-normalised ideal-Bose halo shape using the supplied N_th directly
initial_conditions.source_label: Krstajic et al. 2025 public data, TrapI edd=0.902 0deg, t_hold=300 ms
initial_conditions.description: Published partially condensed Oxford sample represented as a narrow condensate core and broad Bose-enhanced thermal halo.

initial_conditions.parameter_status: published raw-data row with the negative fitted condensate component physically clipped to zero
initial_conditions.source_label: Krstajic et al. 2025 public data, TrapI edd=0.902 0deg, t_hold=4650 ms
initial_conditions.description: Published thermal Oxford sample; the unphysical negative condensate fit is clipped to zero before forward imaging.

initial_conditions.parameter_status: published preparation parameters represented by a contact-TF surrogate
initial_conditions.source_label: Chomaz et al. 2016 elongated 166Er BEC preparation
initial_conditions.description: A common stable elongated erbium BEC baseline; DDI deformation is intentionally absent from the contact-TF generator.

initial_conditions.parameter_status: published trap, atom number, condensate fraction and scattering length used to parameterize a declared subresolution modulation stress input
initial_conditions.source_label: Chomaz et al. 2018 representative lambda=14.3 parameter set
initial_conditions.description: Contact-TF core carrying a weak source-inspired modulation at k_rot=1.61/l_z plus an ideal-Bose halo. This is a negative resolution stress that tests whether the imaging NA suppresses subresolution structure, not a reconstruction or identification of a roton or other many-body state.

initial_conditions.parameter_status: published eGPE benchmark parameters represented by a contact-TF smooth control
initial_conditions.source_label: Chomaz et al. 2019 N=5e4, (145,31.5,151) Hz, a_s=51 a0 theory example
initial_conditions.description: Smooth same-atom-number and same-trap control for separating structured-morphology stress from simple dynamic-range stress.

initial_conditions.parameter_status: published eGPE benchmark parameters with a hand-declared separated three-peak density stress input
initial_conditions.source_label: Chomaz et al. 2019 N=5e4, (145,31.5,151) Hz, a_s=48 a0 theory parameter set
initial_conditions.description: Three separated compact peaks designed only to test recovery of multiple resolved density maxima and associated low-order observables; no phase or many-body-state label is assigned.

claims_boundary.initial_state_status: source-traceable parameters with explicitly declared analytic density generators
claims_boundary.dipolar_physics_status: no nonlocal DDI, kinetic-energy, LHY, three-body-loss or eGPE state solver is present
claims_boundary.phase_status: density-only maps cannot test or establish global phase coherence
claims_boundary.faraday_scale_status: the sealed source inverse uses historical kappa_F=1; this suite can test implementation behaviour under that operator but cannot predict current 166Er Faraday amplitude, SNR or recoverability
claims_boundary.uncertainty_status: one fixed noise realization per condition and fluence is not a coverage statement
